Hey there,

I am Aarya Trivedi

Product Designer ·

I translate ambiguous ideas into structured, intuitive experiences — from early product concepts to scalable workflows. My expertise lies in B2B SaaS and complex systems, where clarity is never a given and every decision carries weight. I work closely with product, engineering, and business stakeholders to design with conviction — and I adapt quickly to new challenges beyond my domain.

A bit about me

Designer driven by
curiosity & people

I'm Aarya Trivedi, a designer driven by curiosity, people, and the systems behind everyday experiences. My work sits at the intersection of UX, service design, and research — where understanding real human behaviour matters more than assumptions.

I enjoy solving problems that live beyond screens: how people move through spaces, make decisions under stress, trust services, or adapt to new habits. That's why I'm drawn to projects in public environments, communities, and products that need thoughtful adoption.

Outside of design, I like exploring new things and trying different experiences.

1.6
Years exp.
5
Industries explored
∞
Curiosity
01
I Notice Everyday Friction
I naturally observe confusing signs, awkward flows, and small moments where experiences can be better.
02
I Love Places With Stories
From city walks to public spaces, I'm fascinated by environments that shape how people feel and move.
03
I Collect Ideas Constantly
Screenshots, notes, packaging, layouts, random thoughts — I'm always gathering inspiration.
04
I Enjoy Meaningful Conversations
Some of my best ideas come from talking to people and understanding how they think.
Timeline

Roles that
shaped me

A timeline of the experiences that shaped how I think about design — from research-led projects to complex enterprise systems.

2024 – Present
2023 – 2024
2023
2021 – 2022
2019 – 2024
Phionike
2024 – Present
Product Designer — Client Engagements
Embedded across two enterprise B2B accounts and one 0→1 product, learning to design inside live, opinionated systems while also shaping new ones from scratch.
Mediaware & Brand Intelle
Enterprise · On-site
  • Working through dense, mature SaaS products where every screen carries years of context — designing with respect for what already exists
  • Restructuring navigation, building new modules, and introducing a design system to bring coherence across long-running surfaces
  • Partnering closely with product and engineering to translate ambiguity into testable flows, not just deliverables
Parent Playbook
0→1 · UK Client
  • Leading design from zero — shaping product structure, behaviour, and tone before there were users to test with
  • End-to-end ownership across research, IA, interaction, and high-fidelity craft
  • Treating early decisions as hypotheses rather than commitments, leaving room to be wrong
Enterprise UX 0→1 Product Design Systems AI Interaction UX Strategy
College Graduation Projects
2023 – 2024
Thesis & Self-Directed Practice
A year of working on problems that lived mostly off-screen — where the design challenge was understanding the system first, and only then deciding what (if anything) to build.
Recycle Right
Thesis · Service Design
  • A long-form study on why apartment recycling fails — and what design can do beyond posters and apps
  • Weeks of field research across housing communities — interviews with residents, building staff, and waste workers
  • Mapped the system end-to-end before touching a single interface; the design followed the research, not the other way around
Unboxing Habba — Gully Tours
Public Engagement
  • Designed engagement layers for live neighbourhood walks — signage, narrative beats, and stops along the route
  • Observed real tours to understand where attention drifted, then prototyped touchpoints to bring it back
  • A reminder that the best experience design often happens off-screen
Service Design Field Research Systems Thinking Behaviour Change
Unikwan
2023
UI/UX Designer
Cycled between fashion, property, and media clients — sometimes in the same week. The constant context-switching taught me to find what was true about each product's audience quickly, and design from there.
  • Led the Fastrack website redesign — rebuilding visual hierarchy and interaction patterns to match the brand's energy
  • Designed UX flows for a property management product, untangling years of feature accretion
  • Shipped a news-sector digital platform centred on readability and reader trust
  • Built reusable UI patterns across projects to hold quality steady under tight timelines
UI Design UX Flows Multi-industry Brand UX
Internships
2021 – 2022
Experience & UI Design
The years where I learned to make small things well — before trying to make big things at all.
TUBB — Experience Design Intern
Chandigarh Architecture Walks
  • Designed visitor journey touchpoints for a programme centred on the city's modernist heritage
  • First exposure to service design as a practice — not just screens, but signage, narrative, and stops along a route
Image Online — UI Design Intern
  • Earliest hands-on design work across applications and websites
  • Built the fundamentals — layout, hierarchy, visual consistency — that everything since has been built on
Experience Design Visual Design Service Touchpoints
Education
2019 – 2024
Srishti Manipal Institute of Art, Design and Technology
Bachelor of Design — Human-Centred Design

Four years that shaped how I see design — as research, as systems, as service, and as craft. Not just visuals on a screen.
